title: Review Restic retention and prune policy
goal: Evaluate the compiled Heim-PC Restic retention policy against actual recovery needs, repository growth and rollback requirements before any forget or prune execution is authorized.

approval.note: Policy review and repository documentation only. No forget, prune, maintenance run, timer enablement or snapshot deletion is authorized.

acceptance.assertion: The exact compiled policy and SHA-256 are recorded together with snapshot age/count/size distributions using read-only repository queries.
Daily, weekly and monthly retention values are justified against realistic restore horizons, outage scenarios and repository capacity.
A non-mutating preview or equivalent explicit candidate report identifies what would be forgotten and what would remain; ambiguity blocks approval.
A fresh exact-snapshot restore proof, repository health check, rollback path and minimum protected snapshots are mandatory preconditions.
The policy is approved, revised or rejected in a reviewed PR; registration does not itself authorize forget or prune.

metadata.operator_authorization: User instruction on 2026-07-11 to implement the next Restic retention review ball; no maintenance, forget, prune, timer enablement or snapshot deletion was authorized.

github_ci_observation.reason: GitHub account payment or spending-limit block; not classified as a code or test failure.

policy_decision.reason: keep-daily protects only one snapshot per calendar day; the old policy would mark four fresh same-day recovery points for removal.

repository_inventory.physical_reclaim_note: Logical restore sizes are not additive physical reclaim estimates because repository data is deduplicated; prune was not executed.

validated_acceptance.policy-inventory: Exact old and candidate policies and hashes, seven snapshot ages, raw repository bytes/blobs and per-snapshot logical sizes are recorded from read-only Restic queries.
validated_acceptance.recovery-model: The candidate keeps all snapshots for seven days, at least the latest fourteen, then fourteen daily, eight weekly and twelve monthly points; the higher storage cost is explicit.
validated_acceptance.preview: Two exact forget --dry-run JSON previews show old keep 3/remove 4 and candidate keep 7/remove 0; ambiguity and unknown snapshots fail closed.
validated_acceptance.safety-gates: Fresh exact-snapshot restore, byte-identical sentinel, repository check, minimum protected snapshot count and both rollback paths passed.
validated_acceptance.review-decision: Infra PR #125 reviewed and merged the conservative source policy; active runtime remains old and disabled, and no maintenance effect is authorized.

validation: Full Infra make validate passed repeatedly, including 30 Restic runtime tests.
The exact published PR #125 head passed again in a fresh detached checkout with clean before/after state.
Policy text and SHA-256 are identical across wrapper, installer, systemd service and runbook.
One review finding was fixed before merge: the nanosecond timestamp regression fixture now remains fresh dynamically.
The live review used only snapshots, stats, forget --dry-run, exact dump and repository check.
metadata.outcome: The unsafe daily-only source policy is rejected. A conservative source policy preserving dense recent recovery points is reviewed and merged, but deliberately not deployed or authorized for maintenance.
metadata.self_review: PASS: PR head, full diff, evidence sidecar, policy hashes, exact restore, repository check, tests, post-state and explicit no-effect boundary were independently re-read.
